steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to emphasize the order in which the two works entered the public domain — which came first, which came second. The notes provide specific years: 2020 for Billy Budd, Sailor and 2021 for The Common Reader. The correct choice must show clear sequence, not just confirm both are now public domain.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Billy Budd, Sailor, a novella by Herman Melville, and The Common Reader, an essay collection by Virginia Woolf, are both in the public domain.: omits the years and implies both entered at the same time. ✗
  • Choice B — The year was 2021, and the copyrights to Billy Budd, Sailor and The Common Reader had finally expired.: references only one year and muddles the sequence. ✗
  • Choice C — Billy Budd, Sailor, a novella by Herman Melville, entered the public domain in 2020, with Virginia Woolf's essay collection The Common Reader following in 2021.: states Billy Budd, Sailor "entered the public domain in 2020" and The Common Reader "following in 2021," establishing unmistakable order. ✓
  • Choice D — Though they once were copyrighted works, Billy Budd, Sailor and The Common Reader are now in the public domain.: like A, omits dates and implies simultaneity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
